evidence.work_arrangement: The position requires office presence at least 3 days per week
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Perform data quality checks, validate transformation logic and support improvements to the analytical layer, escalating issues as appropriate
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD is dominated by data quality, validation, and documentation-for-consistency language throughout responsibilities, with no speed/shipping framing, so rigour dominates.
evidence.domain_risk: State Street Investment Management is a global leader in asset management, entrusted with more than $5.5 trillion in assets.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role sits within a global asset manager supporting reporting, strategy, and sales enablement for a regulated financial-services business, a high-stakes financial reporting context.
evidence.collaboration_width: Strategy; Capital Markets; Technology; sales teams
evidence.jd_authorship: The Analytics Engineer, Officer will serve as a hands-on technical contributor... contribute to ETL pipeline development, data integration, data quality activities and the delivery of scalable analytical products.
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The JD gives specific detail about the team's mandate (MISI, Tableau dashboards, ETL pipelines, single source of truth) reflecting genuine hiring-manager knowledge of the role's scope.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Partner with business and sales teams to understand analytical requirements and develop reporting solutions that support commercial priorities, strategic planning and management decision-making
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role explicitly supports Strategy, Capital Markets, and sales enablement within the business office, a finance/strategic-decision orientation for an asset management firm.
evidence.autonomy_level: Contribute to the development and maintenance of analytical products across the full analytics lifecycle... Support the design, implementation and ongoing maintenance of ETL pipelines
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Nearly every responsibility is framed with contribute/support verbs delivering scoped work within an established analytics lifecycle rather than setting strategy or direction.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Support the design, implementation and ongoing maintenance of ETL pipelines as we build out a single source of truth, helping to centralize, integrate and validate datasets within a scalable analytical infrastructure
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The team is actively building out a single source of truth by centralizing and integrating datasets into existing analytical infrastructure, an extend/consolidate framing rather than a from-scratch build.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Markets Intelligence & Strategic Insights (MISI) is the business unit within the Business Office at State Street Investment Management that supports the organization in reporting & analytics, data infrastructure/quality, sales enablement as well as Strategy and Planning.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: MISI is a named, established business unit with a defined multi-function mandate (reporting, data infrastructure, sales enablement, strategy) within a large global asset manager, indicating a mature function.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI/ML tooling or skill is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Perform data quality checks, validate transformation logic and support improvements to the analytical layer
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Data quality checks are mentioned as a task but framed generically among a list of duties rather than an explicit test-ownership mandate.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: use information to provide a reliable foundation for decision-making, strategy development, opportunity realization and targeted business improvements
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Reliability of information for decision-making is emphasized in a financial-services context, but there is no explicit compliance/regulatory/audit language.
